sections.label: Navigation Bar
sections.instruction: This bar sits at the very top of your portfolio. It shows your name and a button visitors click to reach you.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Your Name or Brand
fields.instruction: This appears in the top-left corner of your site. Use your full name or a short brand you go by.

fields.name: ctaLabel
fields.label: Top-Right Button Text
fields.instruction: The button in the top-right corner. Keep it short — for example: Available for Work, Hire Me, or Let's Talk.

sections.label: Introduction — The First Screen
sections.instruction: This is the very first thing visitors see. Your name appears large and bold, with a short description of who you are. Make it honest and confident.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Your Full Name
fields.instruction: Type your full name exactly how you want it displayed. It will appear as the largest text on your portfolio.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Your Job Title or Role
fields.instruction: What is your main profession? For example: Full-Stack Developer, UX Designer, Data Engineer, or Mobile Developer.

fields.name: tagline
fields.label: One-Line Tagline
fields.placeholder: Turning complex problems into simple, beautiful software.
fields.instruction: Write one memorable sentence that sums up what you do or what you stand for. Think of it as your personal slogan.

fields.name: bio
fields.label: Short Bio
fields.placeholder: 5 years building products at the intersection of design and engineering. Previously at Notion and Linear — now open to new opportunities.
fields.instruction: Write 2 to 3 sentences about yourself. Where have you worked? What do you specialise in? Keep it natural and direct.

fields.name: avatar
fields.label: Your Profile Photo
fields.instruction: Upload a clear photo of yourself. A headshot with a simple background works best. This helps visitors connect with you as a person.

fields.name: location
fields.label: Your Location
fields.instruction: Your city and country. For example: Karachi, Pakistan or Amsterdam, Netherlands. You only need to share a general location.

fields.name: availableForWork
fields.label: Are You Currently Available for Work?
fields.instruction: Switch this ON if you are currently looking for jobs, freelance work, or new projects. A small availability badge will appear on your profile.

fields.name: resumeLink
fields.label: Link to Your Resume or CV
fields.instruction: If your resume is saved online, paste the link here. On Google Drive, go to Share and set it to Anyone with the link can view before pasting.

sections.label: Skills
sections.instruction: List what you know how to do, grouped into neat categories. For example: one group for programming languages, one for design tools, one for frameworks. Add as many groups as you need.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Category Name
fields.instruction: Name this group. For example: Languages, Frameworks, Tools, Design, Cloud, or Databases.

fields.name: skills
fields.label: Skills in This Category
fields.instruction: Type each skill and press Enter to add it. Add as many as you want.

sections.label: Work Experience
sections.instruction: Add the jobs, internships, or freelance projects you have done. If you have no work experience yet, that is completely fine — just turn this section off using the toggle below and it will be hidden.

fields.name: showExperience
fields.label: Show Work Experience on My Portfolio?
fields.instruction: Keep this ON to show your work history. Switch it OFF to hide this entire section if you have not worked yet.

fields.name: role
fields.label: Your Job Title
fields.instruction: What was your official title? For example: Software Engineer, Intern, Freelance Developer, or UX Researcher.

fields.name: company
fields.label: Company or Organisation
fields.instruction: The name of the company or organisation you worked at.

fields.name: period
fields.label: When Did You Work There?
fields.instruction: The start and end dates. If you still work there, write Present. Example: Mar 2022 – Present.

fields.name: location
fields.label: Location
fields.instruction: Where was this job? Write the city, country, or just Remote.

fields.name: description
fields.label: What Did You Do?
fields.placeholder: Led design for the mobile app used by 4M+ users. Reduced onboarding drop-off by 32% through a redesigned first-run experience.
fields.instruction: Describe your main responsibilities and any results you are proud of. Try to mention real numbers where possible. Keep it to 2 or 3 sentences.

sections.label: Projects
sections.instruction: The most important section of your portfolio. Add the best things you have built — personal projects, university work, freelance, or anything you are proud of. Employers and clients look here first.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Project Name
fields.instruction: What is this project called?

fields.name: date
fields.label: When Did You Build It?
fields.instruction: The year or month you finished it. For example: 2024 or April 2024.

fields.name: description
fields.label: What Does It Do?
fields.placeholder: A lightweight project management tool built for solo developers. Used by 800+ people since launch. Built with Next.js and Supabase.
fields.instruction: Describe your project simply. What problem does it solve? Who uses it? Mention any results or real numbers you have. Write 2 to 3 sentences.

fields.name: image
fields.label: Project Screenshot or Preview
fields.instruction: Upload a screenshot or preview image. This is the first visual visitors see for your project — pick your best view.

fields.name: tags
fields.label: Technologies Used
fields.instruction: Type each technology and press Enter to add it.

fields.name: github
fields.label: GitHub Code Link
fields.instruction: Paste the GitHub link so visitors can view your code. Leave this empty if the project is private.

fields.name: webapp
fields.label: Live Website Link
fields.instruction: If your project is live online, paste the link here so visitors can try it.

fields.name: showMembers
fields.label: Was This a Team Project?
fields.instruction: Switch this ON if other people worked on this project with you. A field will appear to add their names.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Team Member Name
fields.instruction: Type this team member's full name.

fields.name: github
fields.label: Their GitHub Link
fields.instruction: Paste their GitHub link so visitors can find them too. This is optional.

sections.label: Education
sections.instruction: Add your academic background — university, college, or any formal degree or diploma. Include it even if you are still studying.

fields.name: degree
fields.label: Degree or Qualification
fields.instruction: What did you study or earn? For example: Bachelor of Computer Science, Diploma in Graphic Design, or A-Levels.

fields.name: institution
fields.label: School or University Name
fields.instruction: The full name of the school, college, or university.

fields.name: period
fields.label: Years Attended
fields.instruction: Start and end year. If still studying, put your expected graduation year.

fields.name: grade
fields.label: Grade or GPA (Optional)
fields.instruction: Add your grade if you want to display it. Leave this empty if you prefer not to share it.

fields.name: description
fields.label: Any Highlights? (Optional)
fields.placeholder: Thesis on distributed caching systems. Dean's List for three consecutive years.
fields.instruction: Optionally add your thesis topic, a key achievement, or main subjects. This is completely optional.

sections.label: Certifications
sections.instruction: Add certificates from online courses or professional programmes — for example from Coursera, AWS, Google, or Udemy. If you have none, switch this section off below.

fields.name: showCertifications
fields.label: Show Certifications on My Portfolio?
fields.instruction: Keep this ON to display your certificates. Switch it OFF to hide this section if you have none.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Certificate Name
fields.instruction: The full official name of the certificate, exactly as written on it.

fields.name: issuer
fields.label: Issued By
fields.instruction: Who gave you this certificate? For example: AWS, Google, Meta, Coursera, or Udemy.

fields.name: date
fields.label: Date Completed
fields.instruction: The month and year you completed this. For example: June 2023.

fields.name: link
fields.label: Verification Link (Optional)
fields.instruction: If your certificate has a public link that proves it is real, paste it here. Visitors can click it to verify. This is optional but builds trust.

sections.label: Contact Information
sections.instruction: How should people get in touch with you? Add your email and any social profiles you are comfortable sharing. This is how employers, clients, and collaborators will reach you.

fields.name: email
fields.label: Your Email Address
fields.instruction: The email address you want people to contact you on. Make sure it is one you check regularly — this is your most important contact detail.

fields.name: github
fields.label: GitHub Profile Link
fields.instruction: Your GitHub profile link. Employers click this to see your code and projects.

fields.name: linkedin
fields.label: LinkedIn Profile Link
fields.instruction: Your LinkedIn profile. This is the most common platform used by employers and recruiters.

fields.name: twitter
fields.label: Twitter / X Profile Link
fields.instruction: Your Twitter or X profile, if you use it professionally. Leave this empty if you do not.

fields.name: showTwitter
fields.label: Show Twitter / X Link?
fields.instruction: Switch this ON to show your Twitter profile link on your portfolio.

fields.name: facebook
fields.label: Facebook Profile Link
fields.instruction: Your Facebook profile link, if you want to include it.

fields.name: showFacebook
fields.label: Show Facebook Link?
fields.instruction: Switch this ON to show your Facebook link on your portfolio.

fields.name: instagram
fields.label: Instagram Profile Link
fields.instruction: Your Instagram profile link, if you want to include it.

fields.name: showInstagram
fields.label: Show Instagram Link?
fields.instruction: Switch this ON to show your Instagram link on your portfolio.

sections.label: Footer
sections.instruction: This short line appears at the very bottom of your portfolio. It is your final sign-off to visitors.

fields.name: text
fields.label: Footer Message
fields.instruction: A short closing line — usually your name and the year. For example: Made with care by Nour · 2024.
